content: Just as the linguistic meaning of "reflexion" evolved from the physical concept of light bouncing back to the cognitive concept of deliberate introspective reflection in human learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can evolve beyond a single step of error feedback to adopt the layered, context-dependent reflective routine that humans use for learning formal skills like academic writing in language classrooms. This frames LLM self-reflection improvement not as a search for new architecture tricks, but as an adoption of the staged reflective habits that have long been taught to human learners.
